op.text: Applied to a six-month ceramics residency at a craft school on the coast on 2025-12-19 (submitted 11:52pm); residency includes studio access, stipend, and cohort; submitted portfolio of pots made fall 2025; described application as 'terrified and I submitted it'; has not told work yet, waiting to hear back before deciding on leave or further reduced hours; described the idea as 'the sanest thing I've considered in ages' versus a year ago when it would have read as 'a breakdown'

[2025-12-20] Speaking about the shift in how she sees work versus identity, Dana said: 'I think I finally understand the difference between what I do and who I am. Design — product work — is what I do. I'm good at it, it pays the mortgage, I don't hate it. But it is not who I am, and I spent fifteen years with those two things fused so tightly I couldn't see the seam. Who I am is closer to — someone who makes things with her hands and looks after the places and people her mother loved. That's not a hobby that got out of control. That's the actual center, and the job is the thing arranged around it now, not the other way around.' She also said: 'It took me all year and a dead mother and a sprained ankle and a boat to get there.'
